meta.title: Mercury Server | HTTP/1.1 in Zig for production
meta.description: Mercury Server is an HTTP/1.1 server in Zig with a hand-written parser, connection pooling, and real-time metrics. Explore docs, benchmark, and contribute on GitHub.
meta.ogDescription: HTTP/1.1 server in Zig focused on performance, predictability, and observability.

hero.title: HTTP/1.1 server with fine-grained memory control and real-time metrics
hero.description: Mercury Server is a low-level backend foundation for teams that need predictable performance, manual parsing, and a clear architecture to evolve in production without framework lock-in.

features.title: Built for systems engineering, not for hiding complexity
features.description: Architecture focused on operational predictability: less magic, more I/O control, explicit limits, and transparent request flow for debugging and evolution.

cards.title: Hand-written HTTP parser
cards.description: No web framework. Direct parsing with configurable header/body limits and robustness-first behavior.

cards.title: Thread-safe pool
cards.description: A concurrent queue decouples accept from processing and reduces thread churn.

cards.title: Built-in metrics
cards.description: The /metrics endpoint exposes request, error, and average latency counters.

cards.title: Baseline hardening
cards.description: Per-connection timeouts, payload validation, and path traversal protection in static serving.

cards.title: Practical operations
cards.description: Run with Zig, Makefile, or Docker Compose in local environments and CI/CD pipelines.

cards.title: Ready to grow
cards.description: Modular Zig codebase ready for keep-alive, file streaming, and advanced observability features.

metrics.title: Official benchmark snapshot
metrics.description: Reference benchmark published in the repository (March 28, 2026, America/Sao_Paulo).
contextItems: Load profile: 4 threads, 64 connections, 8s duration, 3s warmup, 2 rounds.
Tooling: wrk-driven benchmark with automated result artifacts in benchmarks/results.

contribution.title: Contribute with clarity and impact
contribution.description: Whether you are improving parser behavior, observability, tests, or docs, this section gives a predictable contribution workflow.

steps.title: Align scope before coding
steps.description: Read CONTRIBUTING.md and define a narrow, reviewable change with explicit technical intent.

steps.title: Create a focused branch
steps.description: Fork the repository and create a descriptive branch such as feat/parser-header-limit.

steps.title: Run quality gates locally
steps.description: Execute zig build test and related scripts, then capture evidence of successful checks.

steps.title: Open a context-rich PR
steps.description: Document trade-offs, risks, and expected behavior changes so maintainers can review quickly.

panel.title: Community channels
panel.description: Use the right channel for each type of collaboration to reduce review latency.

checklist: Tests and local validations are attached.

footer.description: A low-level HTTP/1.1 server in Zig designed for predictable performance, observability, and practical production evolution.
footer.legalText: Mercury Server is open-source software under the MIT license.
